en: Prescription drugs, the only ovals that can bring people in the Seattle area joy anymore.
ko: 처방약, 이제 시애틀 지역 사람들에게 기쁨을 주는 유일한 것.

en: To put it mildly, to put it mildly, America takes a lot of these things.
ko: 간단히 말해서, 미국은 이런 것들을 많이 복용합니다.

en: 70% of Americans take at least one prescription drug, more than half of us take two.
Researchers say a record 4 billion prescriptions were written in 2011.
ko: 연구자들은 2011년에 기록적으로 40억 개의 처방이 발급되었다고 말합니다.

en: Total drug spending jumped last year by 3% to nearly $330 billion with a B.
ko: 우리의 약품 지출은 작년에 3% 증가하여 거의 3,300억 달러에 달했습니다.

en: Wow. That works out to $1,000 per person on prescription drugs.
Kind of makes you feel like Walter White could have made more money cooking up rheumatoid arthritis medication.
ko: 왈터 화이트가 류마티스 관절염 약을 만들며 더 많은 돈을 벌었을 것 같은 기분이 드네요.

en: But should this really be that surprising? Because it's impossible to escape pharmaceutical ads.
You can't turn on the TV without being subjected to an endless stream of sleep-inducing moths,
ko: TV를 켜면 끝없는 수면 유도 광고나,

en: old men getting boners while varnishing chairs, or this.
ko: 의자에 바니시를 칠하면서 발기하는 노인들, 아니면 이거.

en: If your bladder is calling the shots, you may have a medical condition called overactive bladder, or OAB.
